content.problemStatement:
Write a C program to find factorial of a number using recursion.


Input Format
The only line of input contains a single integer NNN.



Output Format
Print the factorial of the given number.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases (1≤T≤10). Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N (1≤N≤20).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the factorial of N on its own line.

solution.approach: The factorial of a non‑negative integer N is defined as N! = N·(N‑1)·…·2·1 with 0! = 1. Because N ≤ 20, the result fits into a 64‑bit signed integer. A direct recursive function that returns 1 when n ≤ 1 and otherwise returns n*factorial(n-1) computes the answer in O(N) time per test case and O(N) call‑stack depth, which is at most 20. This is well within the limits. The main program reads T, then for each N calls the recursive function and prints the result.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(T·N) total (N ≤ 20).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) recursion stack per test case (≤ 20 frames).
